title: corynotheca divaricata
intro:
Corynotheca divaricata, commonly known as Australian finger lily, is a plant species native to Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, and its health benefits include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Corynotheca divaricata include wound healing, antimicrobial activity, and potential anti-cancer effects. The bioactive constituents of Corynotheca divaricata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The roots of Corynotheca divaricata have been found to contain alkaloids, including flavonoids and saponins, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
